name: Beagle 2
fact: UK-led astrobiology lander aboard ESA's Mars Express. Touched down on Christmas Day 2003 but never made contact. Wreckage was located in 2015 by NASA's MRO HiRISE camera — the lander had reached the surface intact, but two of its four solar panels failed to deploy, blocking its radio antenna. A 12-year mystery solved by orbital imagery.
capability: First UK-led planetary lander; demonstrated that orbital reconnaissance can locate failed surface assets at metre-scale.
